title: Ensure all error messages in Rust follow the guidelines
description:
## Goal
Audit and fix all Rust error messages to comply with the AGENTS.md Error Message Style guidelines.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All `#[display(...)]` messages start with lowercase
- No trailing periods in error messages
- Placeholder names are descriptive (e.g., `{id}`, `{did}`, `{limit}`)
- No `{:?}` debug formatting in user-facing API responses
- All error types use `derive_more::Display` and `derive_more::Error`

## Reference
See AGENTS.md "Error Message Style" section (lines 113-133).

title: Make cargo clippy check for each feature gate dynamic
description:
The nix check in `nix/checks/neoprism-checks.nix` (lines 43-78) manually declares `cargo clippy` commands to check each feature gate one by one:

```nix
# check individual feature if properly gated
echo "checking feature gate for identus-apollo"
cargo clippy -p identus-apollo --all-targets --features base64 -- -D warnings
cargo clippy -p identus-apollo --all-targets --features ed25519 -- -D warnings
# ... (repeated for each feature in each crate)
```

This is brittle and requires manual updates when features change.

**Solution**: Make this dynamic by reading feature flags from cargo metadata (`cargo metadata --format-version 1`). Iterate over each package and its features programmatically in the Nix check, rather than hardcoding individual clippy invocations.

**Affected packages/features**:
- `identus-apollo`: base64, ed25519, hash, hex, jwk, openapi, secp256k1, serde, x25519
- `identus-did-core`: openapi, ts-types
- `identus-did-resolver-http`: openapi
- `identus-did-prism`: openapi
- `identus-did-prism-indexer`: oura, dbsync, blockfrost
- `identus-did-prism-ledger`: in-memory
- `identus-did-prism-submitter`: cardano-wallet
- `node-storage`: sqlite-storage
